item.title: Congrats to Mexico ~ From Canada
item.description: Mexico's 120 million citizens will begin to enjoy free, universal access to healthcare from next year, following a decree by socialist president Claudia Sheinbaum. The landmark policy will unify a fragmented and unequal system that has left many unable to receive care at certain hospitals and clinics, forcing them instead to use only what their insurance provider covers. In theory, Mexico's sprawling network of public health institutions should provide most people with coverage, but in practice poorer people and those in rural communities often cannot get the medicines or treatment they need without out-of-pocket expenses. Private health insurance, meanwhile, fills the gap, creating a two-tier system based on income. The first phase of the new universal system will begin on 13 April, with citizens aged 85 and older eligible to register for the ID needed to access care. From January 2027, healthcare institutions will start working together to bridge gaps in access to care. Since coming to power in 2024, Sheinbaum has sought to undo decades of damage caused by neoliberal policies, building on the work of the previous socialist government. She has pledged to build 1.8m new homes to tackle a housing shortage while strengthening tenants' rights. Last year she announced plans to shorten the work week from 48 hours to 40 hours, while increasing the minimum wage by 13%, continuing a policy of regular hikes championed by her predecessor and mentor Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador since 2018. "For years it was said that the minimum wage couldn't go up," she told a conference in December, "that it would cause inflation, that there would no longer be investment in the country, foreign investment." Despite that, following a cumulative minimum wage increase of 154% since 2018, "we are at a record level of foreign investment," she added. | From YouTube Post: http://youtube.com/post/UgkxERqrpXxcc_UHa0VKAc4LIHWmnkHz2u9r

Also my 13 year old
An important lesson
We got a new couch yesterday and most of today has been spent redirecting claws
I'm a 2 but the knee is much higher
I did not
Talking sh*t is my love language
YES PLEASE
But also "get yo a$s down right now"
Hopefully soon pls
"What's your favorite (thing)"
It's true
And potentially more supportive with less pain
Me with this post
I have used "yo mama" as a retort since he was small and my hope is that he's developed an immunity to it
I miss Wishbone
New life goal
I work from home, so I keep my kids home for all the reasons like fever, vomiting, diarrhea, etc
I bought Radler Grapefruit cause delicious then after 2 decided to Google if it has real Grapefruit juice and it does -.-

item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: Canada’s reputation for being kind and polite has a VERY nasty asterisk
item.description:
I came across the documentary “Two Worlds Colliding,” and I was absolutely horrified. Then I learned about the attempt to subtly cover it up, and I was fuming. I’m just a simple nerd on the internet, but I also have a talent for research. I figured I’d just use that talent by leaving a cache of reliable sources that can’t be easily deleted. . Canada has a reputation of being very polite and kind to people, but there’s a parenthesis left out: Canada is very polite and kind to (white) people. Their treatment of indigenous people is a completely different story. Just like in the US, Canada would gather up native people and send them to abusive reformation schools in order to force them to assimilate. Many children died in these institutions, to the point where there’s even an official search database to look for names of people that were killed in them. There is also an abnormally large number of killed and missing aboriginal women that aren't reported in the news. “The Highway of Tears” is the topic of a niche true crime podcast, despite the fact that these women have not yet been found alive, and more of them are being taken. Amber Tuccaro’s murder investigation was so horrifically botched that the police station publicly issued an apology to the First Nations that was vehemently rejected. Racism in Canada is a deep and complex issue that goes beyond one post, and shouldn’t be told by a Cuban-American in Massachusetts. This post is focusing on a specific type of Canadian police brutality: starlight tours.
During extreme weather, Canadian Police will prowl the streets in search of indigenous people, usually men. When they find one, they randomly arrest them on bogus charges, take their belongings, drive them to a remote location, and leave them stranded there to die of exposure. This usually occurs at nighttime in the middle of winter, hence the name “starlight tour.” However, it can occur at other times as well. Jeremiah Skunk was taken on a tour in summer of 2019. The officer took his possessions, drove him to a random spot, gave him half a bottle of water and a sandwich, and told him to never return to her city or he would be arrested for trespassing. Jeremiah was forced to walk for 10 - 14 HOURS (not miles, hours) towards a nearby reservation in the sweltering heat. He survived by drinking water from puddles. And he was one of the lucky ones. Seventeen year old Neil Stonechild wasn’t as lucky. He was found deceased in the Canadian snow with only a light jacket, jeans, and one shoe.
Police photo of the deceased Neil Stonechild. Cause of death was ruled to be hypothermia. I normally wouldn’t show an image like this out of respect, but in this case, it’s extremely relevant to a situation that would otherwise sound completely outlandish to any sane or decent individual. Seventeen year old Neil was basically stripped and left to die. . The most famous act of Starlight Tours are the Saskatoon Freezing Killings, which includes the death of Neil Stonechild. This is one of the most surreal versions of racism that I’d ever read because the Saskatoon Police Department literally embodied a serial killer - down to killing multiple victims in the exact same way over a period of time. There is literally a timeline of victims.
This was one of the most disturbing timelines I’ve ever read. Like I said, an entire police force literally became a serial killer, down to method of murder and type of victim. You’d think that this was one deranged individual kidnapping people and leaving them in the middle of nowhere to die - not the people that are supposed to protect you.
Dan Hatchen and Ken Munson were sentenced to eight months in prison, and ended up serving six of them. To this day, they maintain their innocence and claim that Neil got drunk and tried to walk home.
See the bottom image. Apparently, this has happened on more than one occasion, but I’m not 100% positive on that. What I know is that if they tried to do it once, they’ll try to do it again when things die down.
The police murdered your loved ones in a shocking act of cold blood that could be the Canadian variation of a southern lynching. I would know, I was born and raised in Texas for 26 years. But I also hope that you know that your loved one, whoever they were, will not be forgotten. If you’d like to share a memory of them on this post, I would LOVE to hear it. And know that I have no intentions of taking this down unless I end up needing to rework it, in which case it will be coming back up. This post, and whatever you share on it, will be on here for as long as Imgur exists.
I included a picture of Neil Stonechild’s body because it was important to show just how brutal a starlight tour is. But I wanted to end on a better note for him. Neil Stonechild was far more than just his death. He was a seventeen year old kid with dreams and a whole world ahead of him. I tried to look for an obituary to learn more about who he was as a person, but couldn’t find much. I did find this from The Saskatoon Star Phoenix. I took out the sentences that referenced his death so this could focus on his life, but as always, the full article will be linked in my sources cited: . “By all accounts, Neil was well-liked. Neil was the second-youngest sibling in the family, which moved to Saskatoon from Manitoba in 1980. Erica [his sister] describes them as churchgoing. Neil was active in both sports and church activities. “Several members of his extended family had been in the Canadian military. Neil, who admired his older brother Marcel, joined the Cadets with him, which Erica said he loved. “Wrestling was a passion. His sister recalls him working hard to improve his skills and even trying to teach friends.” . I looked for pictures and obituaries of the other victims, and couldn’t find them except in a documentary that I couldn’t screenshot. Otherwise, I would’ve included them as well.
The Guardian’s Article on Darrell Night: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/apr/25/darrell-night-who-exposed-canada-police-freezing-deaths-scandal-dies-at-56 Gladue Rights Research Database About The Freeze Killings: https://gladue.usask.ca/node/2860 A Brief Overview of Neil Stonechild’s Death from The University of Toronto: https://jackmanlaw.utoronto.ca/indigenous-perspectives/starlight-tour All That’s Interesting’s Incredible Article on Starlight Tours: https://allthatsinteresting.com/starlight-tours Vancouver Sun’s Article on Neil Stonechild: https://vancouversun.com/opinion/op-ed/michelle-stewart-remembering-indigenous-teenager-neil-stonechild-found-frozen-in-a-field CBC News Neil Stonechild Death Timeline (It Also Includes the Other Deaths): https://www.cbc.ca/news2/background/stonechild/timeline.html Jeremiah Skunk’s Summer Starlight Tour: https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/thunder-bay/gull-bay-starlight-tour-allegations-1.6649168 CBC News Investigation Into Police Coverup: https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatoon/saskatoon-police-starlight-tours-wikipedia-delete-1.3512586 CVT News Article Featured in Screenshot: https://www.ctvnews.ca/saskatoon/article/police-accused-of-deleting-starlight-tours-section-from-wikipedia-page/ The Official Report About the Death of Neil Stonechild: https://www.stonechildinquiry.ca/finalreport/ Saskatoon Star Phoenix’s Article on Neil Stonechild: https://thestarphoenix.com/news/local-news/30th-anniversary-of-neil-stonechilds-death An Article About a Mural Dedicated to Neil Stonechild: https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatoon/neil-stonechild-mural-1.6141287 Neil Stonechild’s Find A Grave: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/43507123/neil_christopher-stonechild “Two Worlds Colliding” - A Documentary About the Saskatoon Freezing Killings: https://youtu.be/M23PGTemqus?si=luTh-TV2nT6hlCJn Here’s an article about Canada’s residential schools: https://allthatsinteresting.com/residential-schools-in-canada The Terrible Handling of Amber Tuccaro’s Murder Investigation: https://www.cbc.ca/missingandmurdered/mmiw/profiles/amber-alyssa-tuccaro About The Highway of Tears: https://highwayoftears.org/

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @rebordacao embroiders t-shirts and I think they're cute! Check out their posts for more.
Man Tax: Colman Domingo Bonus Story: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WFp4N-5R9Tw
“Yes, we can!” - Barack Obama So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Victor Orbán concedes defeat in Hungary election.
item.description:
Centre-right Tisza Party leader Peter Magyar is on course to become Hungary's new prime minister with a two-thirds majority. Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power for a figure in the far-right movement allied with US President Donald Trump.
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán — a powerful far-right figure allied with US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin. — has conceded defeat in the country's election.
Mr Magyar's victory could spell an end to Hungary's adversarial role inside the EU, possibly opening the way for a 90 billion euro ($150 billion) loan to war-battered Ukraine. https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-04-13/victor-orban-concedes-defeat-in-hungary-election/106557060
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: A win for humanity! - CELEBRATION TIME !!!
item.description:
The Hungarians kicked the fascist Orban out. Every bridge, metro station and street is full of people celebrating people.
Orban tried to build in the last 13 years a right wing authoritarian state, with all the power of the state propaganda: like anti-lgbtq+, anti-woke, anit-anitfa, anti-Europe shit and it still didn't work.
This fucker tried to get rid off any democratic structures and every opposition power. Nearly every asshole wannabe dictator around the world, like Trump, Erdoğan or Javier Milei played Orbans playbook. The Project 2025 for example took what Orban has done for the US. That was the reason why Vence was in Hungary, why it was so important for them that the fascist win. That is the reason why his losing is so fucking big. Celebrate!, humanity earned it.
item.dc:creator: onushka

item.title: "Thus ends, after 16 years, the era of Orban, a point of reference for sovereignists and the European far-right, and Vladimir Putin's main ally in the EU. Today's vote was considered the most important of the year in Europe."

item.title: We are the meme. The galaxy is watching.
item.description:
Punisher is a funny superhero because his only powers are Brain Damage and Gun.
Very happy that 2024 rules let fighters cash in their second wind for an ability roll.
Why are players like this?
Why bother living if you never get a second pip?
He saves documents and doesn't afraid of anything.
Oh well. Better try again.
I am always serious, captain. And it is illogical for you to call me "Shirley".
Keep off the roads if you can.
I am enjoying the new season so far.
Not sure why I have so many marvel memes, but if I had to guess, @danat101 is probably to blame.
If you don't think this is super cool then I don't want to know you.
I was a long time glow squid hater until I realized the usefulness of glow sacs when making map walls.
What's the origin of the "which one do I shoot" trope? Is it that one episode of Star Trek? And while we're on that subject, why couldn't Spock just turn the phaser to stun and blast both Kirks?
Why do we call Neil the first man on the moon when he and Buzz were in the same vehicle? Were they not on the moon before they exited the lander? Can I legally transport marijuana through red states as long as I don't step out of my car?
Flesh and Blood TCG tax. RIP Volzar. Long live Volzar.
item.dc:creator: BenjyX55
